meta.title: Saltbound
meta.author: awb_🇨🇭
meta.tone: Honest survival prose. Pacific, anno 1850. A merchantman lost on uncharted reef; one survivor. Salt, heat, patience. The island is harsh but not cruel. There are answers if you walk far enough. No magic that announces itself; only weather, hunger, water, work, and a few old things that the jungle has not finished hiding.

castaway.summary: You took passage from Valparaíso bound for Sydney. The reef found the ship faster than the lookout did. You woke on the beach. Salt in your hair, no boots, no name worth keeping. Survival is a job; you'll learn it like any other.

sailor.summary: You went to sea as a boy, before the schoolhouse. Your hands know rope and your stomach knows weather; you read what charts ask you to read, and no more. Land is the strange place; the sea is your room.

scholar.summary: You were aboard to study the coastal birds of the southern Pacific — a small expedition out of Edinburgh, funded for two years. The birds are still here. The ship is not. You take notes anyway.

cook.summary: You ran the brig's galley. Soup, salt-pork, hardtack — the same three meals in different weather, year after year. You can make a meal from very little, and you can read what's edible by smell better than by sight.

surgeon.summary: You signed on as the Helena's surgeon — two years out of medical school, one chest of instruments, three saved patients on the way over. The chest sank with the ship. The hands are still yours.

shipwreck_beach.name: The Shipwreck Beach
shipwreck_beach.summary: A long beach of pale sand. Behind you, the wreck of the brig Helena — broken on the reef in the small hours of a moonless night, anno 1850. Gulls call. The interior of the wreck still has the sea inside it. North, the jungle. East, palms. West, driftwood. The interior of the wreck is below.
example_descriptions: Salt smell. The sand is hot. A cracked oar half-buried; the ship's name plate, faded gilt, sun-bleached.
A pelican lands ten feet away, considers you, and walks off.
You count the survivors twice. Once: only you. Twice: still only you.

palm_grove.name: The Palm Grove
palm_grove.summary: A stand of coconut palms behind the dunes. Coconuts lie where they fell; one or two hang within reach. The shade is welcome. East, the ground gives way to tidal pools. North, a fruit grove.
example_descriptions: Wind moves the fronds in slow, dry rattles. A crab clicks across the sand.
A coconut at your feet — heavy, hairy, half-buried.

tidal_pools.name: The Tidal Pools
tidal_pools.summary: A flat shelf of black rock, pocked with pools the sea fills and empties. Crabs work the edges. At low tide a hole in the rock shows steps going down to a sea-cave. The path back is west.
example_descriptions: A pool of small fish, bright as glass. A sea-star colour of dried blood.
The sea hisses through a crack twice your height. Below: dark water, cooler than the day.

driftwood_cove.name: The Driftwood Cove
driftwood_cove.summary: A small bay where the current piles wood from a thousand wrecks. Most is rotten; some is good. North, a stand of bamboo grows out of the cliff foot. East, the long beach.
example_descriptions: An oar, a barrel-stave, a hatch-cover. The sea is patient.
A frigatebird hangs unmoving in the wind, then is gone.

bamboo_thicket.name: The Bamboo Thicket
bamboo_thicket.summary: A wall of green canes taller than a man, growing thick out of the cliff base. Some shoots are young and tender, some old and hollow. South, the cove. East, the inland path.
example_descriptions: The wind moves the canes and they knock against each other, slow, like a chime.
A cane snaps cleanly across when you press it just so.

mango_grove.name: The Mango Grove
mango_grove.summary: A clearing of broad-leafed trees heavy with green-and-orange fruit. Wasps. Below, the palm grove and the beach. North, a rougher monkey wood. West, the inland path.
example_descriptions: Fruit drops with a soft thud, every minute or so. The smell is hot and sweet.
An old camp half-claimed by vine: a hammock rotted to thread, pressed leaves still legible in a leather folio. A figure leans over the folio that is not quite there in the bright sun.

jungle_path.name: The Jungle Path
jungle_path.summary: A trampled path through dense undergrowth, kept open by something — a previous castaway or an animal that has lived here a long time. South, the beach. North, the sound of falling water.
example_descriptions: Wet leaves, wet earth, a smell of green rot. Birds you cannot name.
A mark on a tree at chest height — three slashes, old, paled. The kind a person leaves to find their way back.

monkey_hollow.name: The Monkey Hollow
monkey_hollow.summary: A bowl of trees full of small, fast, loud monkeys. They throw what they have. Above, the path climbs to higher ground. South, the mango grove.
example_descriptions: Screams. Wet pulp lands on your shoulder. The troop is watching.
A monkey holds something bright — silver — and is gone before you can see properly.

stream_falls.name: The Stream Falls
stream_falls.summary: A clear waterfall, ankle-deep at its pool, cold even in the sun. Fresh water — the most welcome thing on the island. South, the path. East, the monkey wood. North, the climb to higher ground.
example_descriptions: The pool is so clear you see every smooth stone. You drink before thinking.
A small bird tilts its head at you. It is not afraid; nothing has been here long enough to fear.

hilltop_view.name: The Hilltop View
hilltop_view.summary: A grass-and-rock crest with a view of the whole island and the long curve of sea beyond. The wind is steady here. East, ancient stone. West, an old camp. North, the volcano. South, back down to the falls.
example_descriptions: The sea looks as if you could walk across it. You cannot.
On the highest stone, a figure of salt-light and uniform. He scratches marks on a piece of slate that is not in his hand. He has been here a long time.

ancient_ruins.name: The Ancient Ruins
ancient_ruins.summary: Grey stone older than the island's memory. Steps lead nowhere; a doorway opens onto a wall. In the centre, a carved tablet whose script no living tongue reads. The elder is here when she chooses. East, an old wooden tower.
example_descriptions: Lichen-soft, wind-soft. The carvings are still sharp where the stone was cut against the grain.
A spirit sits with her back to the tablet, weaving something from grasses that aren't there. She does not turn. She is older than the stone.

abandoned_camp.name: The Abandoned Camp
abandoned_camp.summary: A small clearing, a low lean-to, a fire-circle long cold. Tools rest on a stone. Someone lived here a long time and left without packing. East, the hilltop.
example_descriptions: A fishing rod, a bone-handle knife, a pot. An empty wicker cage where a bird once lived. A figure crouches at the fire-circle that is not lit, that has not been lit in years.
The ghost looks up when you come, nods once, and goes back to tending coals that are not there.

lookout_tower.name: The Lookout Tower
lookout_tower.summary: An old wooden tower built by hands you didn't see. The base is dry rot; the climb is sound enough. Above, a platform with a brazier — cold for years. West, the ruins.
example_descriptions: Smells of pitch and old smoke. A bundle of signal rags on a hook, the rags stiff with salt.
From here you could see a ship a long way out. From here a ship could see you.

tower_top.name: The Tower Top
tower_top.summary: A small platform of dry boards. A brazier in the centre, banked with seasalt-encrusted kindling. From up here, you can see the whole horizon. A signal lit here would be seen from very far.
example_descriptions: Wind. Just wind. The horizon is a flat line painted with light.
The brazier holds a heap of dry kindling — old, but the salt has kept it. With a signal-fire kit (try `craft build_signal_kit` — needs 1× sail_cloth + 3× driftwood) it would catch fast and high.
The brazier waits, cold, salt-encrusted. A bundle of dry tinder fits the cradle. The smoke from one good fire here would be seen by any ship within ten miles.

lava_field.name: The Lava Field
lava_field.summary: Black glass underfoot, sharp-edged. The air shimmers. A path of hardened crust threads up toward the volcano's summit. South, the hilltop.
example_descriptions: Heat in waves. Something hard cracks under your boot — volcanic glass, dark as a knife-blade.
A stream of red still moves, slow as honey, somewhere off to the side.

summit.name: The Volcano's Summit
summit.summary: The crater's lip. Inside, the slow, distant glow of red. The wind here is hot one moment and cold the next. From here, every part of the island is small.
example_descriptions: The view is enormous. You feel small the way the sea makes you small.
A hot updraft carries an old smell — sulphur, and beneath it, something herbal, sweet.

shipwreck_interior.name: The Shipwreck Interior
shipwreck_interior.summary: Inside the broken hull, the sea has come and gone many times. A galley, a cabin, a cargo hold half full of water. Useful things still float, if you don't mind getting cold.
example_descriptions: The cargo hold smells of salt and old tar. A floating barrel knocks against your hip.
The captain's cabin is open. His chart-table holds a logbook still legible at the surface, a brass compass, a flare-pistol in a damp leather case.

sea_caves.name: The Sea Caves
sea_caves.summary: Below the tidal shelf, water-carved tunnels go deeper than the light. Strange creatures live here. The tide is the only thing that decides whether you can leave. Up, the rock-pools. Down, deeper.
example_descriptions: Echo of dripping water. Your torch is a very small thing.
Something coils in the dark and is gone. You did not see how big it was. It saw you.

deep_grotto.name: The Deep Grotto
deep_grotto.summary: Deeper than the sea-caves. Cold here, even on a hot day. A hollow of black water and luminous mosses. Something old lives here, and remembers the island before there was an island.
example_descriptions: Pale light from no source you can name. The pool reflects nothing.
A coil — slow, vast — turns once in the dark, and is still.

torn_journal.description: Your old field-journal, salt-warped and missing pages. Some entries survive. They will tell you what to do, if you ask.

coconut.description: A heavy, hairy nut. Crack it for water and meat — once. The shell is useful too.

mango.description: Sweet, fibrous, ripe to the moment. Eat it now or it ripens past you.

cooked_fish.description: Roasted over a fire. Better than raw, both for taste and for not being sick.

raw_crab.description: Pink, sweet, watery. Eat it cooked. Always cooked.

cooked_crab.description: Roasted in the shell. The best food this island has yet given you.

raw_meat.description: Heavy, dark, gamey. The fire will fix it.

cooked_meat.description: Slow-roasted. The smell brought every monkey within a quarter-mile.

fresh_water.description: Cold, clean. Worth more than gold here.

coconut_shell.description: Half a coconut, hollowed and stoppered with bark. Carries water; gives you no excuse to be thirsty.

driftwood.description: Sea-cured, salt-dried. Burns hot once you start it.

bamboo.description: Long, hollow, light. The island's gift to anyone who needs to build.

wood.description: Plain branch wood, dry enough to whittle.

stone_chip.description: A sharp flake. Useful for a thousand things.

crab_shell.description: Thick, plated, hard as iron. Stack three for armour.

sail_cloth.description: Heavy canvas, salt-stiff but sound. Useful if you want to leave.

volcanic_glass.description: Black as truth, edged like a razor. The island's hardest gift.

herb.description: Bitter, pungent, full of small surprises. The botanist will pay.

antiseptic_paste.description: Crushed herbs and rendered fat. Smells terrible. Heals well.

machete.description: Pulled from the ship. Edge will do a few jobs before it bends.

bamboo_spear.description: A cane fitted with a knapped point. Surprising reach. The boars dislike it.

obsidian_dagger.description: A blade of volcanic glass, bound with sinew. Sharper than steel; more fragile.

crab_shell_armor.description: Plates of crab-shell laced with rope. Light, surprisingly tough.

captains_log.description: The brig Helena, out of Valparaíso, bound Sydney. Salt-bloated, but legible at the edges. Last entry, dated 14 March 1850.
captains_log.read_text:
14 February 1850.
Three weeks out of Valparaíso. Fair weather, fair wind. The mate's gout is the worst of our complaints. We are making good time on a southwesterly bearing.

22 February.
Course adjusted thirty points southerly per the merchant's request. The chart is light on detail this far out — we trust dead-reckoning and the lookout. Sails are good; men are well.

01 March.
A bird the lookout cannot name has followed us three days running. Pale, larger than a gull, silent. The men are uneasy. I tell them gulls are gulls. They look at the bird, and not at me.

09 March.
Last sight of any land twelve days behind us. The chart shows open water for two hundred miles in every direction. The lookout sees something on the horizon at dusk that is not there at dawn. The mate believes him.

13 March.
A black line on the southern horizon at noon. By the third bell it is closer; by the fourth it is reef. We change course; the reef is also there to the east. We change again; the reef is to the north. The chart still shows open water.

14 March.
We were not blown here. The island wanted us. The chart shows nothing. The chart is wrong. The men have started seeing the bird again. I have given the order to make for the southerly inlet at first light. May God hold this ship together until then.

— Capt. M. Auer

on_read.text: >>> The captain's hand gets steadier on the page as the entries get worse. As if he was making peace with what he could see.

passengers_diary.description: A small leather-bound diary in a passenger's hand. Tucked inside an oilskin in a lockbox. Half the pages are dry.
passengers_diary.read_text:
20 February 1850.
My first time on a long crossing. I am thirty-two and I have never seen the open sea. The captain is a kind man. The mate is not. The food is what I expected.

28 February.
I write at the rail when the weather lets me. There are five passengers. Mr. R. is older than the captain and reads only one book; Miss V. is the surgeon's wife (she is younger than I am, and braver); the brothers from Lima keep to themselves; the boy in the next cabin is eleven and learning his letters from the bo'sun.

07 March.
A white bird three days now. The lookout will not look at it. I sketch it on this page; the sketch is poor.

11 March.
The mate told me, low, that we are off-course. He would not say by how much. He looked at me as if he had not meant to speak. He has not spoken since.

13 March.
Reef on the southern horizon, then everywhere. The captain shouted very calmly. There is a sound a ship makes that I had never heard. I am writing this in the lockbox. If anybody finds this diary — I was Mary Halloran, of Cork, lately of Valparaíso. I was not blown to this place. None of us were. The island wanted us.

If you read this, traveller — be kind to the others. There are always others. The island remembers them too.

— M. H.

on_read.text: >>> Mary Halloran's hand is small and exact. The pages smell faintly of bergamot under the salt.

compass.description: Heavy, sea-tarnished. The needle still points true. The navigator wants this.

flare_gun.description: Damp, but not ruined. One shot of red light, very high. A ship would see it. It will be a long time before another shot is possible.

ancient_tablet.description: Stone, hand-sized, cool to the touch. The carvings shift when you don't watch them. The elder will recognise it.

map_fragment.description: A torn corner of an old chart. Something was marked on the original — a pencilled X you cannot see whole.

mysterious_idol.description: A small carved figure of dark stone, shape unfamiliar. Cold even in your hand. Older than the ruins.

raft.description: Bamboo and driftwood, lashed with rope, a sail-cloth panel rigged on a spar. It will float. Far enough to be picked up, you hope.

smoked_fish.description: Cured in slow driftwood smoke. Light, long-keeping, dense calories. Provisions for a long walk or a longer sea.

smoked_meat.description: Boar, cured slow over salt-driftwood. Black-edged, dense, travel-ready.

bone_handle_knife.description: Carved from a fish-vertebra, edge of beach-flint. The previous castaway used it for forty years.

worn_letter.description: Sealed in a tin, never sent. 'To Mary, of Falmouth — if this finds you, I did not. The island held me longer than I held myself.' Signed with a single initial: J.
worn_letter.read_text:
To Mary, of Falmouth, post-paid Sydney —

If this finds you, then I did not. I will keep this short, in case the cabin fills.

The ship struck reef in the small hours of a moonless night. The Helena split in two before the long-boat could be lowered. I came through the surf onto a beach that should not be here. The chart is wrong. The chart is wrong about most things.

I lived. For a year I built. For a second year I waited. The third I stopped waiting. The fourth, I think, was when I started speaking to the ghost of the woman who had built the camp before mine — she had been there fifty years, by then. We were good company. I am sorry for the company I am about to be.

The island held me longer than I held myself. Marry again. Tell our son I tried.

— J.

on_read.text: >>> The ghost at the camp watches you read his letter. He nods, very slightly. He has been waiting forty years for someone to read it.

ancient_grimoire.description: A black-bound book on a stone shelf in the deep grotto. The script on the cover is older than any language you know.
ancient_grimoire.read_text:
The first lines are not lines. They are circles, and the circles teach themselves to your eye as you look.

The people who carved this place did not write the way you write. They drew, and the drawings became words while you watched them. By the third page you are reading a language you did not know an hour ago.

The island, the book says, is the lid of a vessel. The vessel holds something the people had no name for, and they did not need a name for it because they knew its shape. They built the ruins to keep the lid on. They left when they were sure the lid would hold without them.

The last page is blank. There is room for one more reader after you. There has been, for as long as the book has existed, room for exactly one more reader.

You close the book. The grotto is colder. You understand the island in a way the elder's spirit said you would.

on_read.text: >>> The carvings on the grotto walls steady. You read them as if they had always been your own tongue. The island is no longer a place. It is a room you have walked into.

signal_kit.description: Three lengths of dry driftwood lashed together with strips of sail-cloth. A flint-striker tucked in the binding. Carry it up the lookout tower, lay it in the cold brazier, and you will not be missed by any ship that passes within ten miles.

foraging.description: Gather plants, fruit, herbs, and small things from the underbrush.

fishing.description: Cast a line at tide-pools, streams, and the open sea.

hunting.description: Track and engage hostile things. The island has more than its share.

firemaking.description: Coax a flame from kindling. Needed for cooking, smoking, brewing, and most cold nights.

cooking.description: Roast fish, crab, and game over a fire. Hot food restores more than raw, and won't make you sick.

smoking.description: Cure cooked food in slow smoke. Smoked provisions are lighter, last longer, and travel further than fresh.

stone_knapping.description: Shape stone and glass into edges and tools.

carpentry.description: Lash wood and bamboo into useful shapes — flasks, spears, armour, and the one thing that gets you off this island.

herbalism.description: The botanist's craft — turn herbs into medicine.

ancient_lore.description: What the island remembers. Granted by the elder when she chooses, not bought.

cook_fish.description: Roast a fish over a fire.

cook_crab.description: Roast crab meat in the shell.

cook_meat.description: Slow-roast boar meat over the fire.

smoke_fish.description: Cure cooked fish in slow smoke. Lighter, longer-keeping than fresh.

smoke_meat.description: Cure cooked meat in slow smoke. Travel-grade provisions.

coconut_flask.description: Hollow a coconut into a watertight flask.

bamboo_spear.description: Lash a knapped point to a length of bamboo.

crab_armor.description: Lace crab-shell plates into a wearable vest.

knap_obsidian_dagger.description: Knap volcanic glass into a wicked edge, bind with sinew.

antiseptic.description: Crush herbs into a strong paste — for any cut the island gives.

build_raft.description: The thing that gets you off this island. Bamboo, driftwood, sailcloth, rope. Built only once.

build_signal_kit.description: Lash dry driftwood with strips of sail-cloth and add a striker. Carry it up the lookout tower's brazier. The smoke will be seen.

wild_boar.summary: Hairy, bristled, surprisingly fast. Tusks. Annoyed by everything.

island_monkey.summary: Small, fast, organised. Throws what comes to hand. Steals what doesn't.

giant_crab.summary: Tide-pool brute, the size of a dog. Pincers harder than they look.

island_panther.summary: Lean, dark, silent. Older than the wreck. The boars get the worst of it; sometimes you do.

sea_serpent.summary: Coil and shadow in the dark water. A long thing. The cave is its home; you are not welcome here.

ancient_guardian.summary: A figure of moss and stone, built around a man-shape that no longer remembers being a man. It does not move until you touch the tablet.

fruit_bat.summary: Larger than the bats you remember. Not aggressive — but in numbers, anything bites.

island_pelican.summary: A big, patient bird. Not friendly, but not your enemy unless you make it so.

navigator_riddle.prompt: I cross without crossing, return without returning, and I bring news without speaking. I never sleep but the moon rests on me. What am I?
navigator_riddle.on_solve_message: The navigator's ghost nods, pleased. 'Right. The sea remembers what nobody writes down.' A map fragment lies on the stone where her satchel would have been — solid, real, waiting for you.

ancient_tablet_riddle.prompt: I outlast the wave but not the stone, am forgotten by all but the wind, and I weigh nothing yet pull armies. What am I?
ancient_tablet_riddle.on_solve_message: The carvings shift, briefly settle, and steady. The elder's spirit turns and looks at you for the first time. 'You read closer than most. Take this — you have earned a piece of it.'

parrot_riddle.prompt: Scratched into the lean-to door, in a child's hand: 'I am hot when wet, cold when dry; I wash, but cannot wash myself. What am I?'
parrot_riddle.on_solve_message: Beneath the riddle, in the same hand, an answer scratched and crossed out and scratched again. The ghost watches you, almost smiling.

kraken_riddle.prompt: Eight arms have I, no shoulders bear them. I write in the dark, but read by none. I drown but cannot drown. What am I?
kraken_riddle.on_solve_message: Something deep down shifts, then quiets. A pale thing rises briefly, leaves a stone-shard at the water's edge, and is gone.

castaways_ghost.summary: A weathered man, half-translucent in the late sun, longer in shadow when the moon is up. He tends a fire-circle that has been cold for fifty years. He notices you about half the time.
dialogue_seeds: Sit. The fire knows its work, even now.
I came in '89. The years stopped keeping separate after the second winter alone.
Two ways off this island, traveller. The signal at the tower — slow, depends on a ship passing. Or the raft. Build it well; the sea took mine.
If you want the signal — `craft build_signal_kit` for the brazier, then climb the tower. The `recipes` command lists what your skills can make.

lines: You see me. Most don't, the first day. Sit, traveller — tell me what brought you to my reef.

choices.label: How do I get off this island?

lines: Two ways. The signal-fire on the tower — gather dry sail-cloth and driftwood, then `craft build_signal_kit`, climb the tower, and the brazier will catch. A ship will see it within a fortnight, maybe.

Or build a raft. Bamboo from the thicket, driftwood from the cove, sail-cloth from your wreck. Lash with rope (`craft build_raft` once you've learned carpentry). The sea is a hard road, but it is yours.
Type `recipes` any time to see what your hands can make.
The island is older than what your captain wrote in his log. The ruins were here long before any ship reached this water.
There is something in the deep grotto under the sea-caves. I would not advise visiting. (You will, anyway.)
The spirit who keeps the ruins was old when I arrived, sixty years past. She is older now. She does not speak much. But she remembers.
If she speaks to you — listen. Few hear what she says.
Drowning. Not from the wreck — that, I survived. From the lake-pool below the falls, twenty years on. I was thirsty, and I was tired.
I stayed to mind the camp. The new ones come every decade or so. Most don't last as long as I did. You might.

botanists_ghost.summary: A wiry, sun-bleached woman who isn't quite there in the bright noon. She bends over pressed leaves that aren't there either. Ink on her fingers — old ink, dry decades.
dialogue_seeds: There are seven medicinal plants on this island. I catalogued five before the fever took me. The sixth is in the lava field. The seventh — never found.
Bring me herbs if you like. I cannot hold them, but I can teach you what they do.
The ink-stain on my fingers won't wash. Some marks last.

lines: Ah — a living one. It has been a while. Sit; the shade is real even if I am not.

choices.label: What is in the lava field?

lines: Wolfsbane — bitter, narrow leaf, violet flower in spring. Crushed: closes a wound. Steeped: settles a fever. Distilled with moonsilver and holy water: the cure for the cursed, in stories that end well.
Moonsilver herb — pale leaf, only in shade, only on stone. Settles the stomach. The seventh plant I never found is, I believe, its night-blooming sister.
Fever. The third week alone. I had drunk all my fresh water by the second and refused to walk to the falls in the rain. Pride and exhaustion in equal measure.
The fever did not last long. The waiting after was longer. I do not recommend it.
On the lava field, where the heat shimmer is heaviest, a small grey-leafed cluster grows in basalt cracks. I named it heat-thistle, in my notebook. The notebook is gone now.
If you find it: do not pick by hand. The leaves leave a burn that lasts a week. Use a folded rag.

navigators_ghost.summary: A tall, thin figure of salt-light in a ship's coat. The brass buttons still catch the afternoon. She watches the horizon for a ship that does not come, that will not come.
dialogue_seeds: I climbed up here to die looking at it. The sea, I mean. Better than below.
Three fragments of the old chart. I had them once. I lost two before the end. They are scattered in the woods now — one rests with the monkey-troop, one with whatever lives in the sea-caves.
If you piece them together, the X is where the temple was. Where it still is.

lines: Ah. You climbed too. Not for the same reason, I hope.
The wind is steady up here. The horizon is a flat line. Most things you wonder about, you can see from this stone.

choices.label: Tell me about the deep grotto.
Why did you climb up here?

lines: We carried two charts. One the captain trusted. One I trusted. They disagreed at exactly the point where we struck reef.

The other chart — older, copied from a Polynesian original two centuries back — showed this island. Showed it in three pieces, like a scattered thing, with an X in the centre. I tore the chart into thirds in my last fever. I would not do it again.
Below the sea-caves there is a grotto. Below the grotto there is a — chamber. The X marks the chamber.

The thing in there is not a god. It is older than gods. It holds the island the way a hand holds a shell. The elder's spirit will tell you to bring back what is there. Listen to her, but do not stay long.
I waited two years. Every dawn I climbed and watched. The third year I stopped climbing, and the fourth I walked up here once more and did not walk back down.
Looking at the sea is gentler than looking at the trees. The trees forget you. The sea pretends to remember.

elders_spirit.summary: An old woman of light and dust, weaving grass that isn't grass. She is older than the ruins. She has been watching this island since there were people on it.
dialogue_seeds: The island remembers in pieces. Bring the pieces.

lines: You return. Most do not. Tell me what you have learned, traveller.
They say: the island held people, once. My people. They left, or were taken. A few of us remained, the way moss remains when the tree has fallen.
They say: the deep grotto was a temple before it was a grotto. What you find there will be older than the temple.
You have brought the tablet. You have read the carvings. You are ready.
I give you what I have. Hold it carefully — it does not leave you, but it changes what you see.

effects.text: >>> The carvings on the walls steady, and you read them as if they had always been your own tongue. The island is no longer a place. It is a room you have walked into.

intro_castaway.title: Saltbound
intro_castaway.description: You woke on the beach. Before night: drink fresh water and light a fire. Before dawn: find what's left of those who came before.
intro_castaway.completion_message:
You know where the water is. You have seen the camp of an earlier survivor — and the ghost who tends it still. You can begin to choose your way out.

Three paths:
  · Signal-fire — `craft build_signal_kit` (1 sail_cloth + 3 driftwood), climb to tower_top.
  · Raft — learn carpentry, then `craft build_raft` (8 bamboo + 4 driftwood + 2 sail_cloth).
  · Lore — bring the ancient_tablet (from the ruins guardian) to the elder's spirit, then find the deep grotto.

Type `recipes` to see what your skills can make. Type `quests` for details on each path.

the_signal.title: The Signal Fire
the_signal.description: Bundle dry driftwood and sail-cloth into a signal-fire kit (`craft build_signal_kit` — needs 1× sail_cloth + 3× driftwood). Carry it up the lookout tower. Set it in the brazier. The whole horizon will see the smoke.
the_signal.completion_message:
You set the signal-fire kit in the cold brazier and strike it. The driftwood catches first, fast; the salt-stiff sail-cloth flares last, hot and white. The flame stands tall against the dark — a column of smoke and light visible to the whole horizon, and farther.

Days pass. Then more days. Then, in the cold grey of one dawn, a sail. A small one at first, then a larger one. They have seen you.

>>> ENDING: RESCUE <<<
You are going home.

the_raft.title: The Raft
the_raft.description: Build a raft (`craft build_raft` — needs carpentry, 8× bamboo + 4× driftwood + 2× sail_cloth). When it's finished, push off. The sea is a hard road, but it is yours.
the_raft.completion_message:
You drag the raft down to the wash-line. The tide is going out. You lash your provisions to the spar, push off, and let the current take you.

The first night, the sea is kind. The second, it is not. The third — you wake to a ship's bell on the western horizon, and a long shadow above the swell, and a hand reaching down for yours.

>>> ENDING: SAIL <<<
You made the road. You are still on it.

the_three_fragments.title: The Three Fragments
the_three_fragments.description: The navigator's ghost speaks of three pieces of an old chart, scattered when she lost them. Assemble all three. The X marks the deep grotto.
the_three_fragments.completion_message: She lays the three fragments on a slate that is not there. They fit. The mark — an X — is at the deep grotto.

the_tablet.title: The Ancient Tablet
the_tablet.description: The elder's spirit speaks of a stone tablet, taken by the guardian when the temple fell. Recover it.
the_tablet.completion_message: She receives the tablet, places it where the chart-table once was, and gestures you to sit. (Talk to her with `talk elder` and pick the 'Teach me the lore' branch.)

the_deep_grotto.title: The Deep Grotto
the_deep_grotto.description: The fragments and the tablet point at the deep grotto, below the sea-caves. Something old waits there. Find it; bring it back to the elder's spirit at the ruins. (You will need ancient_lore to read what the temple says — the elder grants it when you bring her the tablet.)
the_deep_grotto.completion_message:
You return to the ruins with the mysterious idol heavy in your pack. You set it on the lintel where the tablet rests. The carvings on the walls steady. The elder's spirit nods once and looks past you, into a sky that is not the sky.

'The temple has its centre back. The island is whole, for now.'

You understand, for the first time, that this is a place a person can choose to stay. The forest holds you. The sea holds the men who left. You belong to neither, now, and to both.

>>> ENDING: STAY <<<
You are home, in a way you were not before.

the_three_endings.title: Leaving or Staying
the_three_endings.description: Three ways to end your time on this island. The signal-fire on the lookout tower (rescue). The raft pushed off the beach (sail). The lore in the ruins (stay). Each is its own ending. Choose any.
the_three_endings.completion_message: The castaway's ghost has told you the two ways out. The elder's spirit holds the third. The island is patient — choose when you are ready.

effects.text: >>> Anno 1850. The brig Helena, three weeks out of Valparaíso, struck reef in the small hours and broke in two before the long-boat could be lowered. The sea took most of it; the reef took the rest. You washed up here. You are alone, and the island has not noticed you yet.
[Cold, clean water. Worth more than anything you brought from the ship.]
>>> The whole island below you, no bigger than a coin. No other land in any direction. You feel small and very awake.

tropical_storm.title: Tropical Storm
tropical_storm.description: Wind, rain, white-out. Outdoor rooms are harder; the lookout tower is dangerous to climb. Wait it out, or work fast.

narration.on_start: [The wind picks up. The palms bend. Black clouds pile to windward. A tropical storm is coming.]
narration.on_complete: >>> You finished what was needed before the worst of the wind. The storm passes. The island is wetter, kinder for an hour.
narration.on_expire: [The storm has passed. Whatever was loose, blew away.]
